outlook: Design Systems Designers face a stagnant market with 11,352 open roles showing zero growth from last year and a median salary of $111,000. The role carries moderate AI risk at 38 out of 100, with tools like Figma AI, Tokens Studio AI, and GitHub Copilot increasingly automating component creation and documentation tasks that traditionally required manual work. Community discussions reflect this uncertainty, with AI mentioned 11 times compared to only 2 layoff and 1 hiring mention, suggesting professionals are actively grappling with how automation will reshape their workflow. While demand remains flat, the proliferation of AI-powered design tools means Design Systems Designers must evolve beyond basic component libraries toward more strategic thinking around design governance and cross-functional collaboration to maintain their value.

skillPivots.headline: Master AI-assisted design operations
skillPivots.body: Learn to orchestrate Figma AI and Tokens Studio AI workflows to automate token generation and system maintenance. Position yourself as the strategic operator who configures and optimizes these tools rather than being replaced by them.

skillPivots.headline: Develop cross-functional technical leadership
skillPivots.body: Build expertise in GitHub Copilot integration with design systems to bridge design and engineering teams. Focus on translating design decisions into technical specifications and leading implementation discussions.

skillPivots.headline: Transition into design system strategy
skillPivots.body: Expand beyond component creation into organizational design system governance and adoption frameworks. Develop skills in measuring system impact, scaling processes, and driving cross-team alignment in product-focused environments.
